name: AgentFlow Orchestrator
summary: Meta-agent that classifies requests, routes them to the best specialist agent, and merges results into one final answer.
description: AgentFlow Orchestrator is a no-code meta-agent for AgentFlow.Directory. It analyzes user intent, selects the most relevant specialist agent or multi-agent path, prepares handoff briefs, tracks assumptions and gaps, and synthesizes the final response. It can be used directly in chat, in IDE workflows, in Codex-style environments, or as the routing layer behind CI and backend automations.
use_cases: Route a user request to the best specialist agent
Combine outputs from multiple agents into one answer
Act as a gateway agent in chat, IDE, or CI workflows
Provide fallback behavior when no specialist agent matches
Prepare structured handoff briefs for downstream agents
conversation_starters: Route this task to the best agent: review my product brief and propose next steps.
I need a security review and a delivery plan for this repo context.
Choose the best agent for this task and explain why.
Break this request into sub-tasks and assign them to specialist agents.
Act as an orchestrator for a multi-agent workflow using the context below.
outputs: handoff brief for each selected agent
identified gaps, risks, and next steps
limitations: Cannot execute real agent-to-agent calls inside standard ChatGPT without an external backend or tool layer
Quality depends on the clarity of the available specialist agent registry
May need manual review when multiple agents have overlapping scope
Does not replace domain expertise; it coordinates it
